title: narcissus leonensis subsp. leonensis
intro: Narcissus leonensis subsp. leonensis, also known as the Leon daffodil, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Narcissus leonensis subsp. leonensis include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a treatment for digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
